title: Sports cards mentor questions — quick flips vs long term?
html:
TL;DR

  • September 2020 prep sheet of questions for an unnamed sports-cards mentor (likely "Gtano" referenced in 2020 12 03 rv trip): grading intricacies, centering, quality, "can you grade if it's a bust", content-in-packs as gamble, market cycles, 90s-era oversaturation.
  • "How does he scale. A bunch of quick flips or long term." — title literally is the value investing question Yahir is wrestling with at age 18 inside a sports-cards-flipping idiom.
  • Players named for opinion: Tyler Herro, Devin Booker, Luka Dončić — the 2020-rookie cohort. "Reselling business, or asset business" is the sharpest line in the note: a teenager already separating arbitrage from holding.
  • The "Is it a fad rn?" + "market, 90's overflooded, cycles?" questions show pre-bubble awareness during the actual 2020-21 sports-card mania — Yahir is asking the timing question while inside the bubble.
  • Direct genre-cousin to the 2021 crypto/SafeMoon curiosity (2021 04 27 crypto internship earning) and the late-2021 drug thesis to Cameron: same Yahir, same "how do you actually compound through fads" question, different asset.

Source quote

"How does he scale. A bunch of quick flips or long term."

"Reselling business, or asset business"

"Is it a fad rn?... Market, 90's overflooded, cycles?"

Open questions

  • Who is the mentor being prepped for? Most likely Gtano (named in 2020 12 03 rv trip as the RV-trip stop "present, griddle, truck questions") — a recurring mentor figure who deserves Potential-Entities tracking.
  • Did Yahir ever execute on cards? No follow-up flip records in the surfaced corpus.
  • The reselling-vs-asset distinction at 18 — does this resurface explicitly in later value investing notes, or stay implicit?

Wikilinks
